reviews.review_msg: Andrea really engages in the course material. Make sure you read everything front to back. However, she is very particular and looks for very specific things when you write your mid term and final. She has very high expectations, but she does really encourage critical thinking.

I was in the class where half of the people failed and I came out fine.  Andrea just doesn't like palgiarising (or anything she perceives to be close), my suggestion is just to footnote anything and everything that doesn't sound like your idea.  She's really nice, helpful, funny, and interesting.  I would take a class with her again in a heartbeat.
Well, it started off well.  I got good grades.  Then something happened and she failed nearly the whole class.  Was very reasonable and allowed rewrites.  Made me cry first.

He does not allow any technology in class... I was forced to handwrite all my notes then type them after which wasted so much time that I could of been actually studying the notes and material. He was also very particular about how you give test answers. Will never take another class with Joseph.

Like many others, I was intimidated by Professor Taylor on the first day of classes. However as the semester went on I began to look forward to his lectures more and more. He truly cares about his students and has a vast amount of knowledge on the American West. Assigns a lot of readings but they are essential to his tests and assignments.
